findings: The illustrated bird is an Ovenbird: olive-brown upperparts, black-and-dull-orange crown stripes, bold white eye-ring, heavily dark-streaked white underparts, fairly thick pointed bill, pink legs, and short tail all agree with the supplied reference photos and live field-guide descriptions.
Anatomy is coherent: one head, one beak, two legs with feet, one visible folded wing plus the implied far wing, and one tail; no extra or missing structures are visible.
All visible plate text is factually supported by Audubon, including Seiurus aurocapilla, Parulidae, behavior, habitat, field marks, and the stated 14–16 cm / 20–25 cm / 16–28 g measurements.

Measurement caveat: All About Birds currently gives a different length and wingspan range (11–14 cm and 19–26 cm), while Audubon directly supports the plate’s values. This is source variation, not a material plate error.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the plate.
